Key Takeaways:
• When doing Evergreen Launches, there are many tools you may need to make
these work in a more automated fashion.
• There are a couple solid platforms for doing live webinars:
o GoToWebinar - what most people use but is pretty expensive
o Instant Teleseminar - another one that I’ve used, but doesn’t look as good
• For creating that “live webinar look” with a pre-recorded webinar video, there are
many different scripts and tools you can use. These are constantly evolving and
changing, but you can easily find options to suit your needs.
• For list building use ProFollow.com or Aweber (same thing). It’s very easy to create
new lists for each launch - you can do this in 1 minute.
• Or you can use one list and create a new segment within that list - this can get
messy/tricky though so be careful. If you need to combine lists you can contact
support to see if they’re willing to help out.
• For membership sites, look for software that allows you to “drip” out content - you
want it to be based on their join date. This is only applicable if your course is
delivered that way.
o Kajabi, Wishlist, Amember - all of these services do this.
• You can also set up different portals for each launch - but again having the “drip”
feature makes this a lot easier.
• Evergreen is nice in terms of the load on your server, tech support, customer
support, etc. But in terms of handling launches on a weekly basis there is some
additional overhead involved
• Keep in mind, this is not very fun, creative stuff.
• In the long run you shouldn’t focus on these pieces and will easily be able to (and
should) outsource the tasks to a detail-oriented person.
• Focus on running your business and growing it, not the daily details.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now let’s talk about some of the messy technical stuff with doing these Evergreen
Launches. First of all, in terms of platforms, we talked about webinars a few different
times. For doing actual live webinars, the one that most people seem to use is
GoToWebinar. It’s not perfect, but it’s a pretty cool platform. It works a little better on
Windows than it does on MAC, it’s a little more robust on Windows.
There’s also Instant Teleseminar. GoToWebinar is pretty expensive, it used to be
a lot less expensive, but they raised their prices pretty dramatically recently. Instant
Teleseminar is another one that I’ve used and that one, frankly, just doesn’t look quite as
good. It’s like if you’re attending the webinar you don’t get a full screen, you just see a
little PowerPoint. You can only use a PowerPoint; you can’t actually show your screen.
With GoToWebinar you’re just showing your screen, so you can show either a
PowerPoint or you can take them to a web page. There’s lots of different things you can
do. So, Instant Teleseminar doesn’t have all the functionality of GoToWebinar, but it’s a
lot less expensive.
Now, in terms of those evergreen ones, the webinars that are not really webinars,
they just sort of look like webinars and they’re actually videos that are playing that are
meant to look like webinars, there’s lots of different platforms and tools, and also just
simple scripts that you have on your server that will handle all of that. I don’t want to
mention any specific scripts, because they are evolving rapidly.
Just a few months ago there was one or two tools or services, now it seems like
almost every week there’s a new one coming out. So I’m not going to actually mention
any specific of the ongoing evergreen fake webinar type of things, but there are a lot of
them out there and they’re getting better and better. So, there’s good news, they’re
getting better and easier to use.
In terms of list stuff, as I mentioned like with ProFollow or Aweber, either one of
those it makes it really easy. You can create a new list every week or every month for your
launch and just in a couple of minutes you’ve got a new list and bingo you’re all set. Or
you can use one list and basically create a new segment in that list and just mail to the
segments. You’ve got to watch out with those segments, because it’s easier to mess up
and forget and just send an email to the entire list instead of a specific segment. So it’s
probably safer to go with individual lists for each launch.
I know that after a year of doing weekly launches you’re going to have 52 different
lists, so that can get a little cumbersome. If you contact Aweber and ask them to combine
lists, say “Hey, I’ve got these 10 lists and they all joined for the same reason, they’re all
basically the same list, can you combine them?” They’ll do that. They don’t officially say
that they’ll do that, so it’s not an official stance, but whenever I’ve contacted them with
that request they’ve done it for me, so I assume they’ll do it for you as well. Again, it’s
something you just contact support and ask them if they’ll do that.
As far as member platforms, if you’re doing a membership site like the way
Product Launch Formula is a membership site, you’ve got a portal for Product Launch
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Formula, if you’re doing a portal you want to look for software that allows you to
basically “drip,” that’s what they call it, to drip out content. So if someone joins then they
get certain content released to them based on their join date.
Now, if you’re doing a portal or membership site where they get all the content in
it immediately when they join, then you don’t have to worry about it. But, if you’re doing
something like Product Launch Formula where we walk people through and release a
module every X number of days, then you’re going to either have to have software that
automatically will drip it out, and Kajabi will drip it out, I’m not sure but I think Wishlist
will drip it out, aMember, I don’t know if it does it out of the box but there’s ways that it
can be adapted to do it. Or you can basically set up a different membership site.
Say your program is like five weeks long. You can set up five instances, five
different versions. So basically on week one what you would have is five different installs
– or to save space on my whiteboard let’s just say you have a three-week long program
and you have three different installs. So these are actually whatever, this is your
membership site portal, so then basically what you would do is here’s launch number one
and you put them in here.
These people basically walk through and you’re going to walk them through three
weeks. The first week they get the first module, the second week they get the second
module, third week they get the third module. So, then launch two can go into this portal
and obviously launch three goes into this portal.
Let’s say today is June 1st and we just do this launch, so these folks are in here
and they get module one. Then let’s say it’s June 7th and this launch they’re going into
here and they’re on module one now on June 7th. These other folks are actually now on
module two on June 7th, because this is their second week so you’ve given them the
second module.
Now let’s say it’s June 14th and you do this launch, so these folks are now on
module one, these folks are on module two, and since they’ve been in for three weeks
these folks are on module three. Then you do launch number four and basically on
launch number four on June 21st you’re actually just putting them back into here, right?
It’s that simple.
So you can do it on a manual basis or you can get something that drips out
content like some of those sites I mentioned before.
Okay, now there’s always a little bit of messiness that you’ve got to figure out.
Like what do you do with comments? When they’re coming into the portal do you leave
old comments, do you not leave old comments, do you take the date off the old
comments? So there’s always a little messiness, but there’s always ways to work around
that stuff.
Then finally, with staffing, one of the beautiful things with the evergreen is that
you smooth a lot of your lead flows, your sales, so it makes it easier to plan your
customer service if you’re getting X number of sales every week instead of 500 sales and
then no sales for a year, then 500 more sales. So evergreen is nice in terms of the load on
your server and the load on your customer service, the load on your tech support,
anything like that it can be good.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
But, because of the way there’s juggling here and when I showed you the weekly
launches some of the creating news lists and sending out the content, there is some
additional overhead there in terms of stuff that has to happen on a weekly basis and stuff
that has to happen accurately. You don’t want to screw up and when they’re supposed to
get the first piece of Prelaunch Content be mailing them the sales letter or something like
that.
So there is going to be some additional overhead just logistical stuff, not really
fun creative stuff either, it’s the kind of stuff that personally I don’t love to do, but that’s
the type of stuff that’s pretty easy to outsource. You get some detail oriented people that
can follow a process - and it is all about turning it into a process and you want to have
checklists and you’re going to want to have standard operating procedures, but basically
you have an ongoing process. It can be documented very carefully and then you have to
have people that can take care of that.
Generally, this is maximum of a one-person job to manage this and it’s probably
not even that, it’s probably once you’ve got it really dialed in it’s more like a half-person
job or a quarter-person job to handle these processes. It’s not rocket science, but in the
long run it’s probably not something you as an entrepreneur are going to love doing. Just
something to think about.
So that’s some of the nitty gritty stuff that you have to keep in mind as you go to
Evergreen Launches.
